column4: The past passive participle of √vac “say”
This is a pronominal form and is inflected like sárvaḥ.
This is a pronominal form and is inflected like sárvaḥ.
More often used as a pronominal adjective meaning “one’s own”; sometimes used as a reflexive pronoun (“oneself”). It is inflected pronominally, like sárvaḥ.
Note the usage with a dative: tasmai rōcatē “it pleases him”
Note that this verb is ubhayapadi, taking ātmanēpadam endings when the action benefits the agent, and parasmaipadam endings otherwise.
Note that this verb is ubhayapadi, taking ātmanēpadam endings when the action benefits the agent, and parasmaipadam endings otherwise.
In the ātmanēpadam, it means to “enjoy” (usually sexually), with a complement in the instrumental (less often locative); in the parasmaipadam, it generally means to “wait” or “stay.”
The thing from which one “ceases” is put in the ablative (pañcamī).

column3: he/she/it makes sense, stands to reason, is possible
he/she/it occurs suddenly or unexpectedly, falls into trouble, turns out poorly
